title: templategraph-php output graph (v1)
description: Schema for the JSON document produced by `templategraph scan --format json`. Identified at runtime by `schema_version: 1`. This schema is frozen: every object uses `additionalProperties: false`, and any change to the wire shape — breaking or non-breaking — is published as a new `output-graph-vN.schema.json` rather than an in-place edit. Existing schema files (including this one) are never modified after release, so consumers can pin to a specific version with confidence.

schema_version.description: Major version of this output schema. Increments only on breaking changes; a new schema file is published alongside each new major version.

id.description: Stable identifier for the node within this graph. Edges reference nodes by this id.

kind.description: Node category. `entry` = top-level entrypoint chosen by the user or auto-discovered (coincides with `is_entrypoint == true` in stable output). `php_template` = file reached via include from another node. `unresolved` = include target that could not be statically resolved (dynamic argument or missing file).

display_name.description: Human-readable label, typically a path relative to the project root for resolved nodes, and a free-text description for unresolved nodes.

is_entrypoint.description: Whether this node was treated as an entrypoint. Distinct from `kind == entry` only in transition states; in stable output, `is_entrypoint` is true iff `kind == entry`.

absolute_path.description: Absolute filesystem path. Omitted for unresolved nodes (which have no on-disk location).

root_relative_path.description: Path relative to the project root passed via `--root` or auto-inferred. Omitted when the node lies outside the root or has no on-disk location.

from.description: id of the source node (the file containing the include directive).

to.description: id of the target node (the included file or unresolved placeholder).

kind.description: PHP-level include directive that produced this edge. NOTE: an asymmetry exists in v1 — edges to missing-file `unresolved` nodes preserve the original PHP kind (`include` / `require` / etc.), while edges to dynamic-argument `unresolved` nodes use `unresolved`. Consumers needing a uniform 'is this edge resolved?' check should use the target node's `kind` field. The asymmetry is tracked for review and may be normalized in a future schema version.
